jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
Great River Arts seeks an administrator who is a detail\-oriented and self\-sufficient individual for the position of Executive Director at the GRA center, located at 122 First St. SE, in the historic district of downtown Little Falls.

Great River Arts (GRA) is a nonprofit, member\-based organization, governed by a volunteer board that provides multidisciplinary art experiences in greater Minnesota. The executive director supports the president and reports directly to the board. This position is responsible for overseeing staff, contractors/vendors and volunteers delivering GRA programs and services all in conjunction with GRA staff and board.

**Position Summary**: Under the direction and guidance of the Board of Directors, the Executive Director is the chief staff leader responsible for managing the human and financial resources of the organization to achieve the mission and goals of GRA. The Executive Director oversees fundraising and development, administration, programming, marketing, and the arts center facility.

**Reports to**: GRA Board of Directors

**Supervises**: Staff and volunteers

**Hours and Salary**: 40 hours/week. Exempt. $50\-59,000/yr.

**FUNDRAISING AND DEVELOPMENT (40%)**

* Grant Management \- Research, write, and submit grant proposals for the organization. Implement and ensure compliance of all grants funds. Provide grant reports to the Board of Directors.
* Donor Relations \- Develop, strengthen, and maintain donor relationships and records.
* Sponsorship \- Secure corporate and local sponsorships to fund programs and operations.
* Campaign Oversight \- In conjunction with the Board of Directors, oversee fundraising campaigns for GRA’s annual appeal and other fundraising events.
* Membership Development \- Develop and coordinate annual membership drive and other membership initiatives to augment membership of the organization.
* Volunteer Management \- Develop and implement volunteer recruitment strategies. Maximize the use of volunteers to further the mission of the organization.
* Strategic Partnerships \- Identify, initiate, and expand partnership opportunities that advance the mission of GRA.

**Administration (25%)**

* Daily Operations \- Provide oversight to the daily work of the organization. Develop and implement needed policies and procedures.
* Financial Management \- Manage the annual budget by overseeing daily bookkeeping. Paying the bills of the organization. Signing contracts with vendors. Maintaining bank accounts and transactions. Ensure compliance with all grant funding and nonprofit regulations. Provide accurate reporting on financials to the Board of Directors.
* Risk Management \- Oversee insurance and vendor contracts.
* Personnel Management \- Hire, supervise, train, evaluate, and, as necessary, discipline or fire staff.
* Strategic Vision \- In conjunction with the Board of Directors, create, collaborate, and implement the strategic vision and mission of the organization.
* Facility/Building oversight\- assess and manage the needs for the building. Coordinate with contractors and Board of directors to care for the upkeep of the building.

**Programming \- (15%)**

* Programming Oversight \- In conjunction with Programming Assistant, collaborate and execute mission driven programming.
* Schedule Management \- oversee and manage the calendar of events to ensure the facility is available for programming, performances and events.
* Committee Collaboration \- Work in conjunction with established committees to provide quality programming, performances, and events. Ensure committee recommendations align with the mission of the organization. Provide administrative support for organizational committees. Volunteer, Special Events,Building Maintenance, GRTV, Development and Membership.
* Public Representative\- Act as the public face of the organization on major organizational events, networking opportunities, and community functions.

**Marketing \- (15%)**

* Communication \- Develop and steer the overall communication plan to ensure cohesive messaging and brand consistency for electronic newsletters, social media, retail vendor updates, and website content.
* Material Development \- design, develop, and market material related to fundraising and development efforts of the organization.
* Website \- Manage and update the website to ensure a user friendly experience.

**Other Duties as Assigned by the Board of Directors \- (5%)**

* Cleaning/Organization
* Etc…

**Qualifications:**

* Bachelor’s degree in related field or commensurate experience.
* Proven experience in nonprofit management and administration.
* Demonstrated success in grant writing, fundraising, and member and donor development.
* Strong communication, public speaking and relationship building skills.
* Ability to work independently and manage multiple projects.:
* Demonstrated financial management experience.
* Experience with QuickBooks Online accounting software and nonprofit fund accounting.
* Knowledge of the Minnesota arts community and funding landscape.
* Experience managing team performance, hiring, training, and scheduling personnel.
* Willingness to push the organization forward with an open mind.
* Commitment to the mission and goals of GRA

**Skills and Abilities**

* Effective oral and written communication skills.
* Ability to work independently and as a team to meet deadlines.
* Strong organizational, interpersonal skills.
* Ability to exercise discretion and independent judgement.
* Has a creative eye for space planning and desgn.
* Ability to work effectively with a wide range of constituents.
* Commitment to coworkers and to the mission of Great River Arts.
* On\-time and dependable.
* Availability to work evenings and weekends based on event schedule.

**Benefits:**

* Paid Time off.
* 6 Days of ESST.
* 11 Paid Holidays.

Pay: $50,000\.00 \- $59,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Flexible schedule

**EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR**

**PITTSBURGH, PA**




**ORGANIZATION:**

Medical Rescue Team South Authority (MRTSA), founded in 1976, provides health care to patients in the prehospital setting. As a part of the health care delivery system, MRTSA’s services help maintain a healthy and productive society. With a great emphasis on the compassion and skill of its trained staff, MRTSA is continually seeking ways to share its professionalism with the community at large. MRTSA is committed to the continuous improvement of preventive and dynamic health care services in ways that will contribute to a seamless transition of patients through the continuum of care. To learn more, please visit: http://www.mrtsa.com/home.


**Position Summary**


Medical Rescue Team South Authority seeks an accomplished, mission\-driven Executive Director to serve as the organization’s chief executive and senior administrative leader. Reporting directly to the Board of Directors, the Executive Director is responsible for the strategic, financial, and administrative leadership of MRTSA and for ensuring that the organization has the people, resources, systems, partnerships, and governance support needed to deliver reliable, high\-quality emergency medical services to the communities it serves.


The Executive Director oversees organizational planning, finance and budgeting, human resources and labor relations, Board support, vendor and contract administration, process improvement, and community and stakeholder relations. The Executive Director works in close partnership with other MRTSA leaders to align operational performance, workforce strategy, and fiscal stewardship with MRTSA’s mission and long\-term sustainability.




**Core Responsibilities**

* Serve as the chief executive officer of MRTSA and principal administrative partner to the Board of Directors.
* Lead strategic planning and annual organizational goal setting; translate Board direction into executable plans, priorities, and performance expectations.
* Prepare and present annual operating and capital budgets, multi\-year forecasts, and regular financial reports to the Board; monitor revenues, expenses, cash position, capital needs, and budget\-to\-actual performance.
* Oversee financial administration, including internal controls, audit support, procurement, contract administration, vendor oversight, and coordination with billing, legal, accounting, technology, and other third\-party partners.
* Partner with operational leadership to establish and monitor service, workforce, safety, quality, and financial performance metrics; use data to identify risks, improve processes, and strengthen organizational performance.
* Lead human resources administration for the organization, including staff supervision, performance management, investigations, policy administration, and labor relations, and support collective bargaining, grievance processes, and discipline consistent with law, policy, and the applicable CBA.
* Build and maintain strong working relationships with the Board, partner municipalities, hospitals, county and regional EMS/public\-safety partners, regulators, vendors, and community stakeholders.
* Support sustainable revenue and organizational resilience through reimbursement oversight, grant development and administration, membership/subscription growth, municipal and community partnership development, and other mission\-aligned funding opportunities.
* Ensure MRTSA policies, procedures, and administrative practices are current, well\-documented, compliant, and operationally effective.
* Represent MRTSA professionally at meetings, public events, and interagency forums.




**Required Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree in business administration, public administration, healthcare administration, finance, management, or a related field. Significant EMS Leadership Experience may be substituted for degree qualifications by the search committee.
* At least five to ten years of progressive leadership experience in EMS, healthcare, public safety, municipal government, nonprofit management, or another complex 24/7 service environment.
* Demonstrated success in budget development, financial reporting, forecasting, and executive\-level decision support.
* Experience leading staff, supervising managers, and navigating employee relations in a unionized or similarly regulated environment.
* Experience working directly with a governing board, committee structure, or elected/public\-sector stakeholders.
* Experience managing vendors, contracts, and cross\-functional organizational operations.
* Excellent written, verbal, and interpersonal communication skills, including the ability to present clearly to the Board and external stakeholders.
* Valid Pennsylvania driver’s license, or the ability to obtain one within a defined period after hire.




**Preferred Qualifications**

* Master’s degree such as an MBA, MPA, or MHA.
* Familiarity with Pennsylvania EMS, ambulance reimbursement and billing, municipal authorities, public\-safety partnerships, and grant administration.
* Experience leading organizational change, process improvement, workforce stabilization, and culture\-building initiatives.
* Prior experience in a mission\-driven healthcare or public\-service organization with multiple revenue sources and capital\-planning needs.




**Compensation:**

* $125,000\.00 to $140,000\.00 dependent upon qualifications and experience

**To Apply:**

Please send cover letter and resume to:


Search Committee


Medical Rescue Team South Authority


315 Cypress Way


Pittsburgh, Pa. 15228




ALL CORRESPONDENCE IS DUE BY CLOSE OF BUSINESS ON August 7, 2026\.

jobs.title: Executive Director of Philanthropy
jobs.description:
The Director of Philanthropy provides strategic and operational leadership for the Heart of the Valley YMCA’s Annual Campaign, individual annual giving, corporate philanthropic support, event sponsorships, donor stewardship, volunteer fundraising, and related revenue\-generating initiatives.


Reporting to the Chief Mission Advancement Officer, the Director develops and executes the organization’s Annual Giving Campaign and additional year\-round fundraising strategies designed to increase donor acquisition, retention, engagement, and giving. The position manages a portfolio of individual and corporate donors and prospects, cultivates new philanthropic relationships, and stewards existing supporters by meaningfully connecting them to the YMCA’s mission and community impact.


The Director leads the Association\-wide Annual Campaign and works closely with branch leaders, staff, board members, and campaign volunteers to strengthen a culture of philanthropy throughout the organization.


Corporate partnership responsibilities are specifically centered on generating charitable revenue through Annual Campaign gifts, event sponsorships, and other approved mission\-aligned giving opportunities. This includes identifying and soliciting new corporate partners, renewing and growing existing partnerships, coordinating sponsorship fulfillment, and stewarding corporate donor relationships.


The Director serves as a visible ambassador for the Heart of the Valley YMCA and helps connect individuals, businesses, volunteers, and community leaders with opportunities to advance youth development, healthy living, and social responsibility throughout the community.

* Monitor corporate giving, sponsorship revenue, renewals, and relationship activity to support long\-term partnership growth.

**Donor Stewardship and Recognition**

* Develop and implement comprehensive donor stewardship and recognition strategies that strengthen donor engagement, retention, and long\-term philanthropic support.
* Ensure timely acknowledgment, impact reporting, recognition, and meaningful opportunities for donors to experience the YMCA's mission.
* Develop renewal and upgrade strategies based on donor interests, giving history, and engagement.

**Volunteer, Board, and Staff Engagement**

* Recruit, train, support, and recognize Annual Campaign volunteers while overseeing the Association's volunteer campaign structure in collaboration with branch leadership and advisory councils.
* Equip board members, volunteers, and staff with the tools, training, and resources necessary to identify, cultivate, solicit, and steward donors.
* Develop framework and lead training of all branch and department leaders in campaign planning/execution, storytelling, volunteer engagement, and building a culture of philanthropy.
* Coordinate staff\-giving initiatives and encourage broad organizational participation in fundraising efforts.
* Maintain a visible and collaborative

jobs.title: Deputy Director, Internal Leadership Communications
jobs.description:
**The Foundation**


We are the largest nonprofit fighting poverty, disease, and inequity around the world. Founded on a simple premise: people everywhere, regardless of identity or circumstances, should have the chance to live healthy, productive lives. We believe our employees should reflect the rich diversity of the global populations we aim to serve. We provide an exceptional benefits package to employees and their families which include comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage with no premiums, generous paid time off, paid family leave, foundation\-paid retirement contribution, regional holidays, and opportunities to engage in several employee communities. As a workplace, we’re committed to creating an environment for you to thrive both personally and professionally.

**The Team**


The Communications Division, led by the Chief Communications Officer, oversees the foundation’s global communications strategy. The division brings together internal and external communications to advance the foundation’s mission to create a more equitable world.  

  

The Internal Communications team connects employees to the foundation’s mission, strategy, work, and impact. We partner with leaders and teams across the organization to develop strategic, employee\-centered communications that inform, engage, and inspire. Through thoughtful planning, channel management, leadership communications, storytelling, and enterprise\-wide communications coordination, we help employees understand what matters most, feel connected to our global community, and contribute to a more informed, aligned, and engaged organization.**Application Deadline: Friday, August 14, 2026**

**Your Role**

Reporting to the Director of Internal Communications, you oversee the foundation’s internal leadership communications portfolio—building the systems, strategies, and capabilities that enable leaders across the foundation to communicate with clarity, empathy, and purpose. You are a trusted advisor to senior leaders and a skilled strategist who understands that how leaders communicate is as important as what they communicate.


You develop and manage the frameworks, tools, channels, and content that equip the Executive Leadership Team, and leaders at all levels to engage authentically with employees—during moments of strategic importance, organizational change, and day\-to\-day connection. You are also a resident expert and coach for the team on internal communications platforms, data, and analytics. You help architect and steward the foundation’s internal communications technology ecosystem—including SharePoint and Viva Engage—and you use data and analytics to continuously improve the reach, relevance, and impact of leadership communications. You bring a systems thinker’s mindset to both platform architecture and communications strategy, ensuring the foundation’s channels and content work together to support a coherent and effective employee experience.


As a key member of the Internal Communications leadership team, you model strong judgment, creative problem\-solving, and the ability to lead decisively through ambiguity. This role is designed as a potential succession path for the Director of Internal Communications and offers significant scope, visibility, and opportunity to grow into enterprise\-wide communications leadership.

* Develop and deliver regular reporting on internal leadership communications effectiveness for the Internal Communications Director and Communications Division leadership.
* Partner closely with the Deputy Director, Employee Experience and Engagement to ensure leadership communications are integrated appropriately into employee engagement channels and initiatives.
* Collaborate closely with the Office of the CEO, People function, Foundation Strategy Office, and External Communications to ensure alignment and consistency across leadership messaging and enterprise\-wide communications moments.

jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
jobs.description:
POSITION SUMMARY
  
  

Laguna Healthcare Corporation, a tribally chartered nonprofit healthcare organization serving the Pueblo of Laguna in Laguna, New Mexico, is seeking a Chief Executive Officer (CEO) to lead the organization through its next phase of growth and help shape its long\-term strategic direction. The organization provides comprehensive primary care, behavioral health, pharmacy, rehabilitation, and preventive health services to the community it serves.
  
  

Position Summary
  
  

The Chief Executive Officer will work closely with Board of Directors to deliver quality healthcare services while executing its long\-term strategic plan. The CEO will assist the Board of Directors in setting the future path of the organization. This position is responsible for the day\-to\-day operations of the Corporation, including oversight of a School Based Health Center, Community Health Clinic, financial management, and programming and community engagement. This position is also tasked with expanding LHC’s services to include a dental clinic and other specialty services such as Obstetrics/Gynecology, Maternal Health, Audiology, Podiatry, Nephrology and Pediatrics.
  
  

**Direct Reports:**  

Chief Operating Officer
  
  

Chief Medical Officer
  
  

Chief Financial Officer
  
  

HR Director
  
  

Executive Assistant
  
  

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  
  

* Develop and implement the strategic plan and vision set by the Board.
* Prepare activity reports, and work with the Chief Financial Officer (CFO) to prepare financial reports for the monthly meetings of the Board of Directors.
* Assist with interpretation of Federal, State, Tribal and local rules and regulations.
* Determine personnel requirements and ensures that properly qualified individuals occupy each position.
* Ensure the development of HR programs for the successful recruitment, placement and development of qualified personnel.
* Reviews and gives performance evaluations, makes salary recommendations, and resolves personnel problems beyond the responsibility of supervisory staff.
* Prepares required grant applications and budgets to ensure sound financial management and an optimum efficiency of operations.
* Maintains diligent monitoring of all financial activity through the use of a system of responsible accounting, including budget and internal controls, and an annual independent audit.
* Ensures that problems and exceptions noted in the annual audit are thoroughly analyzed and that correcting solutions are implemented.
* Develops RFPs for Operational and Clinical delivery systems and other professional services.
* Develops information necessary to apply, negotiate and enter into other funding agreements such as Federal, State and County grants, and non\-profit funding sources.
* Oversees contractors and/or consultants to ensure timely completion of any and all contract activities including budgets and related deliverables.
* Maintains prescribed security controls to protect the Corporation against criminal and fraudulent acts and unnecessary risk or exposure.
* Participates in any required training for all staff and Board members.
* Is present when the Board of Directors provides reports, presentations, and updates to the Governor and/or Council of the Pueblo of Laguna.
* Participates, or is represented, in community, State and national associations and professional activities which define the delivery of health care services and aid in both short and long range planning of health services and facilities.
* Provides a public relations/community engagement program.
* Pursues a continuing program of formal and informal education in health care, administration, and management areas to maintain, strengthen and broaden his/her concepts, philosophy, and ability as a health care administrator.

Education/Certification

* Skilled in effective organizational and program management
* Strong time management skills and ability to manage operations efficiently and cost effectively
* Ability to oversee the development of new protocols, policies, and procedures

jobs.title: Director of External Affairs
jobs.description:
JOB SUMMARY


In 2028, the Olympic and Paralympic Games are coming to the United States, and Team OKC will play a vital role in that global moment. As the organization responsible for delivering Canoe Slalom and Softball competitions in Oklahoma City, Team OKC will showcase the innovation, hospitality, and spirit of the American people while creating an unforgettable experience for athletes and fans alike. Delivering the Olympic Games is a team effort—one that requires creativity, collaboration, and exceptional leadership to bring the vision to life.


The Director of External Affairs will lead Team OKC’s strategy for engaging state and local governments, municipal partners, chambers of commerce, economic development organizations, civic leaders, and regional international consulates to support successful delivery of the Games in Oklahoma City. Reporting to the President of Team OKC, this role is responsible for building strategic partnerships, advancing integrated municipal planning, and ensuring alignment among public\-sector stakeholders, LA28, and Team OKC functional areas. This role will work closely with government agencies, civic leaders, regional organizations, and community partners to ensure seamless coordination and long\-term regional engagement.


ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS

* Develop and execute Team OKC’s external affairs strategy to build and maintain strong relationships with federal, state, tribal, regional, and local government entities.
* Serve as the primary liaison between Team OKC and elected officials, government agencies, municipalities, chambers of commerce, economic development organizations, and regional stakeholders.
* Coordinate closely with LA28 Government Relations and Public Affairs teams to ensure alignment on governmental engagement strategies and initiatives.
* Foster productive partnerships with civic organizations, business leaders, nonprofit organizations, and international consulates to advance Team OKC's objectives.
* Collaborate closely with the Greater Oklahoma City Chamber Government Relations Team to align legislative priorities, coordinate advocacy efforts, leverage existing government relationships, and ensure a coordinated approach to advancing Team OKC's strategic objectives and the successful delivery of the Games.
* Facilitate cross\-functional collaboration with Team OKC departments to support venue operations, transportation, public safety, permitting, infrastructure, and community engagement initiatives.
* Coordinate governmental approvals, agreements, and interagency communications necessary to support planning and operational readiness.
* Represent Team OKC at public meetings, community events, governmental forums, and stakeholder engagement opportunities.
* Monitor legislative, regulatory, and policy developments that may impact Team OKC planning and operations.
* Develop briefing materials, presentations, reports, and correspondence for executive leadership and external stakeholders.
* Support crisis management and issue resolution involving governmental or community partners as needed.
* Build and maintain positive relationships that strengthen Team OKC's reputation throughout the region.
* Perform other duties as assigned.
* Bachelor’s degree in public administration, Political Science, Public Policy, Communications, Business Administration, or related field preferred.
* Ten or more years of experience in government relations, public affairs, public policy, economic development, external affairs, municipal planning, or related stakeholder engagement environments, including support for complex public\-sector initiatives, major events, community projects, or public\-private collaborations.
* Experience working with state and local governments, public agencies, chambers of commerce, economic development organizations, civic leaders, and cross\-functional teams; Olympic, Paralympic, major event, tourism, or large\-scale community project experience is a plus.
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills, strong organizational and self\-management abilities, and the ability to travel independently with reliable transportation and a valid driver’s license.
* Ability to lift and carry up to thirty pounds.

jobs.title: Director of Development and Communications
jobs.description:

Building on a strong foundation of donor support, community partnerships, and public trust, the Director will drive growth in philanthropic revenue, elevate Legal Aid's visibility and influence, and strengthen relationships with individual donors, foundations, corporations, and government partners. The Director will also lead a talented Development and Communications team while partnering closely with the Executive Director, Board of Directors, and community stakeholders to advance Legal Aid's impact and ensure long\-term sustainability. Responsibilities will include:

jobs.title: Director of Development
jobs.description:
**About Friends of Waterfront Park**

  

Friends of Waterfront Park (Friends), a nonprofit organization, was established in 2012\. City officials and civic leaders recognized that, like all great parks in cities across the country, Waterfront Park needed a nonprofit leader to ensure its lasting success. From our early days of facilitating public engagement around the design of Waterfront Park to our historic fundraising Campaign for Waterfront Park and our development of public programs and public safety strategies centered in compassion, Friends has been a key partner to the City of Seattle and communities across the city in an unprecedented effort to build an extraordinary urban public space.

  

In 2025, the Waterfront Park Grand Opening Celebration brought more than 50,000 people to celebrate a new era for Seattle’s central waterfront. With the $170M Campaign complete and Waterfront Park open to the community, Friends is focused on welcoming everyone to this iconic Seattle gem all year through fundraising, programming, public safety investments, park experience, and long\-term stewardship.

 **Our Mission** affirms why we’re here: to bring people and local communities together to ensure Waterfront Park thrives as a vibrant gathering space that fosters connections, and honor Seattle’s natural beauty and stories.

 **Our Vision** imagines where we’re headed. Seattle is transformed by Waterfront Park, a public space sparking joy, connection, and belonging with every visit.

 **Our Values** of Community, Equity, Care, Collaboration, and Adaptability ground how we work, from programming and partnerships to public safety and storytelling.

  

Friends of Waterfront Park recognizes and acknowledges historic and existing systemic racism embedded in our city. Friends commits to being an anti\-racist organization by prioritizing racial equity within the organization and in the public spaces we operate.

 **Land Acknowledgement**

  

Waterfront Park is situated on the land of the Coast Salish Peoples, who have resided here since time immemorial and continue to thrive. With respect and humility, we acknowledge the history of the waterfront, the dispossession of land from the Coast Salish people and, most importantly, the strength and resilience of Native people and their culture through this history and to the present.

 **Position Summary**

  

Friends of Waterfront Park is seeking a strategic, relationship\-driven **Director of Development** to lead and grow its philanthropy revenue program. Reporting to the CEO, the Director of Development will oversee and grow a **$3\.66M annual fundraising portfolio** and lead a philanthropy team spanning major gifts, leadership giving, events, sponsorships, grants, annual giving, and database functions. Serving as a member of the Senior Leadership Team, the Director contributes to organizational strategy, alignment, and culture while advancing Friends’ philanthropic vision. This is a role for an experienced fundraiser who brings both the relationship skills to personally manage major donor portfolios and to develop and execute ongoing donor cultivation and stewardship strategies. They also bring the management breadth to provide strategic oversight of a full function development team. They are energized by the challenge of building systems, developing people, and stewarding major donor relationships simultaneously.

 **This is a full\-time, exempt, hybrid role based in Seattle, WA.**

 **The Opportunity**

  

Friends of Waterfront Park is a growing organization with a total operating budget of $9\.3M, now entering an exciting new chapter following the completion of its historic $170M capital campaign.



The Director of Development will lead an experienced philanthropy team through a significant transition: modernizing development operations and data systems, building out a comprehensive annual giving and grants program, refining the events strategy, and stewarding the major donor relationships that are foundational to Friends' long\-term success.

  

The Director will have meaningful visibility with the Board of Directors and the Development Committee, working in close partnership with the CEO to align fundraising strategy with organizational priorities. Friends' signature fundraising events, the Pier Party and Dinner with Friends, anchor the summer calendar and represent significant donor engagement opportunities the Director will help shape.

 **The Fundraising Portfolio**

  

The Director of Development will lead strategy and relationship management across Friends’ fundraising program. While the 2027 portfolio may adjust in scope, the 2026 portfolio totals approximately $3\.66M in annual fundraising and includes:

  

* Leadership Circle: $1,200,000 (33%)
* Fundraising Events (Key events include Pier Party, Dinner with Friends): $990,000 (27%)
* Major Gifts: $700,000 (19%)
* New Grants / Institutional Funding: $600,000 (16%)
* Annual Giving and Board Giving: $172,500 (5%)

  



The Director will personally manage a portfolio of approximately 100 top donors and prospects. The top 25 relationships will be co\-managed in close coordination with the CEO and Board; the remaining 75 donors and prospects will sit in the Director's independent portfolio. The Director will also staff and support the Development Committee.

  

In addition, the Director will provide stewardship oversight for previous campaign contributions ($2,000,000\) and secured grants ($1,853,238\) currently in the budget. Candidates should be prepared to engage with donors and funders across the full scope of Friends’ philanthropy revenue.

  

**Responsibilities**

 **Fundraising Strategy and Leadership**

  

* Lead and grow Friends’ philanthropy revenue program across major gifts, leadership giving, annual fund, institutional funding, and events.

**NeuroPong Executive Assistant and Program Coordinator**

**Position Overview**

NeuroPong is seeking an energetic, highly organized, dependable, and mission\-driven *‘Executive Assistant and Program Coordinator’* to work directly with our Founder and CEO, Dr. Antonio Barbera, in supporting the day\-to\-day operations of our rapidly growing nonprofit organization.

This is a unique opportunity to gain hands\-on experience in nonprofit leadership, healthcare innovation, community engagement, event management, and program development while helping improve the lives of individuals living with Parkinson's disease, multiple sclerosis, dementia, stroke, traumatic brain injury, and other neurological conditions through the transformative power of table tennis.

The *Executive Assistant and Program Coordinator* will play an important role in helping NeuroPong (http://neuropong.org) expand its programs, strengthen community partnerships, coordinate events, and support organizational growth. No two days are the same, making this an ideal position for someone who enjoys variety, takes initiative, and thrives in a dynamic, purpose\-driven environment.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Provide administrative and organizational support to the Founder and CEO in the day\-to\-day operations of NeuroPong.
* Assist with scheduling, communications, meeting coordination, correspondence, travel arrangements, and other administrative tasks.
* Help maintain participant, volunteer, event, and organizational records through data entry and use of online databases and software systems.
* Provide support for NeuroPong programs, events, fundraising activities, marketing initiatives, community outreach, and special projects as needed.
* Assist with event preparation, logistics, registration, equipment, and follow\-up for classes, demonstrations, conferences, and community events.
* Help monitor ongoing projects, organize information, and ensure timely follow\-up on action items.
* Perform other administrative and operational duties as assigned in support of NeuroPong’s mission.

**What We Are Looking For**

* Strong organizational, communication, and interpersonal skills.
* Dependable, self\-motivated, and able to work independently while managing multiple priorities
* Proficiency with Microsoft Office, Google Workspace, and common online software platforms; willingness to learn new systems.
* Comfortable with data entry and maintaining accurate records.
* Interest in nonprofit organizations, healthcare, community service, or program management.
* Valid driver’s license and reliable transportation for occasional local meetings and events.
* Ability to occasionally lift and carry up to 50 pounds and assist with event setup.
* Willingness to work occasional evenings or weekends for special events.
* A genuine enthusiasm for NeuroPong’s mission and a desire to make a positive impact.

**Time Commitment**

* Part\-time position (approximately 10 hours per week).
* Flexible schedule.
* Hybrid work environment with a combination of remote work and in\-person meetings with the NeuroPong founder in Fort Collins, CO.
* Opportunity for increased responsibilities and additional hours as NeuroPong continues to grow.

**Compensation**

**$25\.00 per hour**

**Why Join NeuroPong?**

This is an opportunity to become part of an organization that is redefining neurorehabilitation through movement, joy, and human connection.

As the *Executive Assistant and Program Coordinator,* you will work closely with the Founder and CEO, gaining firsthand experience in nonprofit leadership, healthcare innovation, strategic planning, community outreach, fundraising, program development, marketing, and organizational growth. You'll build meaningful relationships with healthcare professionals, researchers, community leaders, and individuals living with neurological conditions while contributing to a mission that is improving lives around the world.

If you're looking for a position where your work truly makes a difference, where you'll develop valuable professional skills, and where you'll have the opportunity to grow alongside an innovative international nonprofit, we'd love to hear from you.

**Contact**

Antonio Barbera, NeuroPong Founder and CEO, info@neuropong,org

Pay: $25\.00 per hour

Benefits:

* Flexible schedule

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Fort Collins, CO 80524

jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer (COO) – Construction & Home Services
jobs.description:
**About Green Attic**

Green Attic is a growing home\-services company serving residential and commercial customers throughout the Chicagoland area.

Our operations include roofing, insulation, energy\-efficiency improvements, remodeling, restoration, and multi\-trade construction projects.

Ownership has built a strong sales pipeline and revenue engine. We are now hiring an experienced Chief Operating Officer to lead company\-wide execution, strengthen management, improve profitability, and build the systems required for continued growth.

This is not primarily a field\-supervisor or production\-manager position. The COO must understand construction operations deeply, but the main responsibility is to lead the business at an executive level.

The right person will translate company goals into operating plans, develop department leaders, manage financial and operational performance, and ensure the organization executes without depending on ownership for routine decisions.

**Position Overview**

The Chief Operating Officer will oversee day\-to\-day business operations across production, project management, scheduling, customer service, warehouse, purchasing, administration, and field execution.

This leader will work directly with ownership to establish priorities, allocate resources, improve organizational structure, and maintain accountability across departments.

The COO must have significant construction or home\-services experience and understand how projects move from sales and estimating through scheduling, production, billing, completion, warranty, and customer closeout.

This position requires executive judgment, financial discipline, leadership maturity, and the ability to build a scalable operating system across multiple brands and service divisions.

**Executive Responsibilities**

* Translate ownership’s vision and growth goals into clear operating plans, priorities, budgets, and measurable targets.
* Lead company\-wide operations across roofing, insulation, remodeling, construction, customer service, scheduling, warehouse, and administrative functions.
* Develop and manage department leaders, project managers, production managers, supervisors, and support teams.
* Establish clear organizational structure, decision\-making authority, reporting relationships, and accountability.
* Own operational performance, including revenue execution, gross margin, labor efficiency, project completion, customer satisfaction, and cash\-flow coordination.
* Review financial and operational reports to identify risks, missed targets, waste, and growth opportunities.
* Build annual, quarterly, and weekly operating plans with clear owners, deadlines, and scorecards.
* Create a consistent management rhythm using leadership meetings, KPI reviews, departmental scorecards, issue tracking, and documented follow\-up.
* Ensure sales commitments are properly transferred into estimating, scheduling, production, billing, and project completion.
* Improve communication and coordination between sales, production, project management, warehouse, accounting, and customer service.
* Identify leadership gaps and recruit, train, coach, or replace team members when required.
* Establish performance expectations and hold managers accountable for results, not activity or excuses.
* Lead major operational decisions involving staffing, capacity, scheduling, subcontractors, equipment, materials, vendors, and expansion.
* Reduce operational dependence on ownership by creating reliable leaders, systems, and escalation procedures.
* Evaluate new services, markets, technologies, and operational investments based on profitability and execution capacity.
* Protect the company from operational, safety, compliance, customer\-service, and financial risk.

**Construction and Production Oversight**

The COO will not personally manage every project or crew. However, this person must understand construction well enough to challenge managers, identify weak planning, evaluate risk, and determine whether operations are being run correctly.

Responsibilities include:

* Oversee production performance across roofing, insulation, remodeling, restoration, and multi\-trade projects.
* Ensure project scopes are reviewed for completeness, constructability, labor needs, material needs, scheduling risks, and margin protection.
* Confirm that departments have proper systems for job readiness, crew assignments, material coordination, change orders, documentation, quality control, and closeout.
* Review major job delays, callbacks, warranty issues, safety incidents, customer disputes, and margin losses.
* Visit active projects as needed to verify management reports and assess operational performance.
* Ensure field and production leaders maintain quality, safety, cleanliness, documentation, and customer communication standards.
* Identify recurring field problems and require permanent process corrections rather than repeated emergency fixes.

**Financial and Performance Management**

* Own and improve operational profitability across all service divisions.
* Partner with ownership and accounting to establish budgets, forecasts, labor targets, and margin expectations.
* Review project\-level and departmental financial performance.
* Track gross margin, labor cost, material cost, subcontractor cost, callbacks, warranty expenses, write\-offs, and outstanding receivables.
* Identify unprofitable services, crews, workflows, vendors, or project types and implement corrective action.
* Improve job costing, purchasing controls, change\-order management, billing readiness, and project closeout.
* Ensure managers understand how their decisions affect company profitability and cash flow.
* Build scorecards that provide ownership with a clear view of performance without requiring constant operational involvement.

**Systems and Organizational Development**

* Build and improve SOPs, workflows, checklists, approval systems, reporting processes, and accountabi

· Implement financial controls and understand the accounting impact and results of operating decisions. Periodic reporting of results to the CEO and board of directors.

**Position Overview**


The Director of Development provides strategic leadership for City Year Rhode Island's comprehensive fundraising program, driving philanthropic revenue growth and long\-term financial sustainability in support of the organization's mission. Reporting to the Executive Director and serving as a member of the Senior Leadership Team, the Director develops and executes an integrated fundraising strategy that includes individual giving, corporate partnerships, foundation and government grants, special events, donor stewardship, and Board engagement. The Director serves as a key external ambassador for the organization, cultivating high\-impact relationships with donors, funders, corporate leaders, and community stakeholders while partnering closely with the Executive Director and Board to achieve ambitious fundraising goals.**Job Description**


The Director of Development is responsible for leading and executing City Year Rhode Island's comprehensive fundraising strategy to advance the organization's mission and long\-term financial sustainability. Reporting to the Executive Director and serving as a member of the Senior Leadership Team, the Director leads a diversified fundraising program that includes individual giving, corporate partnerships, foundation and government grants, donor stewardship, fundraising events, and Advisory Board engagement.


Working in close partnership with the Executive Director, Advisory Board, and City Year's national development team, the Director cultivates strategic relationships that generate philanthropic investment and deepen community engagement. The Director develops and implements data\-informed fundraising strategies, manages a robust donor pipeline, and strengthens the systems, partnerships, and fundraising capacity needed to achieve annual revenue goals and advance City Year Rhode Island's strategic priorities.


As a key external representative of the organization, the Director builds and maintains strong relationships with donors, funders, corporate and community partners, and civic leaders. This role is responsible for fostering a culture of philanthropy across the organization and ensuring fundraising efforts align with City Year's values, strategic plan, and commitment to service and educational equity.

CLEVELAND PLAY HOUSE



POSITION: Director of Development



REPORTS TO: Managing Director



DEPARTMENT: Development



STATUS: Full\-Time, Exempt



SALARY RANGE: $105,000–$120,000, commensurate with experience

  



ABOUT CLEVELAND PLAY HOUSE



Founded in 1915, Cleveland Play House (CPH) is America’s first professional regional theatre and a nationally recognized cultural institution. Throughout its history, CPH has launched groundbreaking new work, produced celebrated classics and contemporary plays, and served as a vital artistic home for generations of artists and audiences.

  



As Cleveland Play House enters a transformative new chapter, the organization is building on its legacy while embracing new opportunities for artistic excellence, community impact, and long\-term sustainability. The Director of Development will play a critical leadership role in shaping that future.

  



POSITION SUMMARY



The Director of Development serves as Cleveland Play House’s chief fundraising strategist and development leader, responsible for planning, implementing, and advancing comprehensive fundraising efforts that support the organization’s mission, artistic ambitions, and financial goals.

  



Reporting directly to the Managing Director and serving as a member of the senior leadership team, the Director of Development will cultivate meaningful relationships with donors, foundations, corporations, and community partners while developing strategies to increase contributed revenue and deepen philanthropic engagement.

  



This position is ideal for a strategic, relationship\-driven fundraiser who is energized by building partnerships, inspiring investment, and helping guide a historic institution through a period of growth and evolution.

Strategic Fundraising Leadership


* Develop and implement comprehensive annual and multi\-year fundraising strategies.
* Establish contributed revenue goals and benchmarks in partnership with the Managing Director and Finance team.
* Identify opportunities to grow major gifts, institutional support, sponsorships, annual giving, and future campaign initiatives.
* Serve as a strategic thought partner to the Managing Director regarding fundraising priorities, donor engagement, and institutional advancement.
* Provide regular revenue forecasting and fundraising analysis to support organizational planning and decision\-making.



Major and Principal Gifts


* Manage and grow a portfolio of approximately 75–100 individual donor prospects ($10,000\+).
* Cultivate, solicit, and steward major and principal gift donors.
* Develop individualized strategies for donor engagement, renewal, and increased investment.
* Partner closely with the Managing Director, trustees, and volunteers on major gift cultivation and solicitation efforts.
* Prepare donor strategies, briefing materials, proposals, and stewardship plans.



Foundation and Corporate Giving


* Develop and execute strategies for foundation, government, and corporate support.
* Manage relationships with institutional funders and corporate partners.
* Work with grant\-writing support to ensure timely submission of proposals and reports.
* Identify new institutional funding opportunities aligned with organizational priorities.



Annual Giving and Donor Stewardship


* Provide strategic oversight of annual giving programs and donor communications.
* Ensure thoughtful and meaningful stewardship experiences across donor levels.
* Collaborate with Marketing and Communications staff to develop fundraising messaging and donor\-facing content.
* Strengthen donor retention, renewal, and upgrade strategies.



Board and Volunteer Engagement


* Partner with the Managing Director and Board leadership to advance fundraising efforts.
* Support Development Committee activities and fundraising initiatives.
* Assist trustees in identifying, cultivating, and soliciting prospective donors.
* + Help foster a culture of philanthropy throughout the organization.



Team Leadership


* Supervise and mentor development staff.
* Establish clear goals, priorities, and accountability measures.
* Foster a collaborative, donor\-centered, and mission\-driven culture.
* Support staff professional growth and development.



Organizational Leadership


* Serve as an active member of the senior leadership team.
* Participate in institutional planning and cross\-departmental collaboration.
* Represent Cleveland Play House at community, donor, and civic events.
* Support organizational initiatives that advance audience engagement, community relationships, and institutional visibility.

  



QUALIFICATIONS



Required Qualifications


* Bachelor’s degree or equivalent combination of education and experience.
* Minimum of five years of progressively responsible fundraising experience.
* Demonstrated success securing six\-and seven\-figure major gifts.
* Experience developing and implementing fundraising strategies that achieve measurable results.
* Strong relationship\-building and donor cultivation skills.
* Excellent written, verbal, and presentation abilities.
* Ability to manage multiple priorities and work effectively in a fast\-paced environment.
* Strong organizational and project management skills.
* Ability to work evenings and weekends as required for donor events, performances, and organizational activities.

* Seven or more years of fundraising leadership experience.
* Experience in performing arts, higher education, cultural, or nonprofit organizations.
* Experience with campaign planning and execution.
* Knowledge of Cleveland’s philanthropic community.
* Experience managing fundraising staff and volunteers.

jobs.description:
Description:

**POSITION SUMMARY:**


The Development Director for Catholic Schools provides strategic leadership and support to advance development efforts across Catholic schools in the Diocese of Charlotte. Working closely with school leaders, pastors, advancement teams, and diocesan leadership, this role helps strengthen a culture of philanthropy rooted in the mission of Catholic education.


This position partners with schools to support fundraising, donor engagement, and development planning while helping build long\-term capacity for sustainable growth. Through collaboration, coaching, and strategic guidance, the Development Director will help advance the vitality and future success of Catholic education throughout the Diocese.

**KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:**

* Plans and directs quarterly regional or systemwide workshops for school presidents, principals, development staff, or key school fund development volunteer leaders.
* Meets on site regularly with each of the 20 individual schools to assess needs, set annual giving goals, and strategize for continued growth.
* Works closely with schools as they develop and carry out annual school fund development plans, aligning with school operations, advancement strategy, and systemwide Catholic school priorities.
* Works with schools to focus on both major gifts and planned gifts.
* Provides training for staff and volunteers regarding the solicitation of gifts.
* Provides strategic direction in data reporting for optimal use of donor database management systems.
* Gives direction on expanding the donor base and increasing donor engagement.
* Creates templates for school marketing, such as annual fund brochure and giving guide.
* Gathers key annual fund data from each school on a quarterly basis for systemwide analysis.
* Designs and facilitates professional development and peer learning opportunities.
* Leads Diocese of Charlotte participation for \#iGiveCatholic.
* Meets regularly with Development Office staff and Catholic Schools Office staff with reports on the individual schools’ activities and results. · Attends staff meetings as requested.
* Supports Catholic schools in carrying out fund development efforts in other ways as needed.

**OTHER RESPONSIBILITIES:**

* Performs other duties as required by the Deputy Director of Development and requested by the Superintendent of Schools.
* Coordinate work closely with the Director of Advancement for Catholic Schools on school\-facing advancement priorities.

**DIRECT REPORTS:**

* N/A


Requirements:
**POSITION REQUIREMENTS:**


Education

* A bachelor’s degree in marketing, communications, or a related field.


Experience

* Minimum of 5 years of experience in development and/or fundraising. Preferred experience with annual fund growth, donor relations, major gifts, planned giving, or campaign support.
* Strong understanding of Catholic school mission, advancement, stewardship, and relationship\-based fundraising.


Skills

* Self\-motivated and able to plan, organize, and coordinate development activities in a timely manner.
* Strong written and oral communication skills.
* Proficient in Microsoft Office. Knowledge of fundraising database software and Canva experience preferred.
* Work effectively to build trust with staff, volunteers, donors, and the public.


Physical


The physical requirements described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the Responsibilities of this job. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the position’s Responsibilities.

* The position requires the ability to sit or stand for prolonged periods; frequent keyboard use/repetitive hand motion; occasional reaching/pushing/pulling/working overhead; occasionally lifting/carrying up to 25 lbs.

**WORKING ENVIRONMENT:**

* Responsibilities involve travel within the Diocese of Charlotte and some professional development, and work beyond the regularly scheduled workday.

**ABOUT THE DIOCESE OF CHARLOTTE:**


The Roman Catholic Diocese of Charlotte encompasses 2,750 personnel across 93 parishes and missions and 20 Catholic schools in 46 counties of western North Carolina, with a growing Catholic population of more than 565,000\.

new_jobs.description:
**Director of Communications and Executive Affairs**
----------------------------------------------------

**Department**  

Communications and Executive Affairs **Reports to**  

Chief External Impact Officer **Location**  

Oakland \- Regional Support Officer **Type**  

Full\-time **FLSA Status**  

Exempt **Schedule**  

Full year  

### **About KIPP NorCal**


We are a thriving nonprofit network of 23 free, public charter schools open to all students. At KIPP, we believe all children should grow up free to create the future they want for themselves and that schools can and should be a critical factor in making that vision a reality. Together with families and communities, we create joyful, academically excellent schools that prepare students with the skills and confidence to pursue the paths they choose—college, career, and beyond—so they can lead fulfilling lives and build a more just world.


Our student community consists of over 7,100 elementary, middle, and high school students in East Palo Alto, Oakland, San Francisco, San Lorenzo, San José, Redwood City, and Stockton. 80%qualify for free or reduced price lunch,27% are multilingual learners, and17% receive special education services. We strive to cultivate a representative team of teachers and leaders that reflect our students’ diversity.

### **Role Overview**


The Director of Communications and Executive Affairs is responsible for the design and high quality execution of cohesive, impactful communications and collateral that reinforce KIPP NorCal’s brand identity and strategy across internal and external audiences. They set direction on all visual and print communications and messaging, ensuring stakeholders have the right information at the right time, thus strengthening their effectiveness, trust, and affiliation with KIPP.


This role also oversees best\-in\-class board governance systems, structures and processes; CEO communications, visibility, and support; key systems that support a strong Regional Support Office (RSO) culture; and systems to ensure excellent communication and coordination around our 2030 vision and goals, and how those translate to annual initiatives and key results.


The Director of Communications and Executive Affairs will report to the Chief External Impact Officer, and is a member of the RSO’s leadership team. They lead a 3\-person team, owning the department’s strategy, budget, and results.

### **Core Responsibilities**

 **Communications, Messaging and Brand Positioning**

* Develop and steward KIPP NorCal’s organizational narrative, brand, and communications strategy across internal and external audiences, ensuring KIPP NorCal speaks with a consistent, high\-impact voice across every audience and channel.
* Leverage a deep understanding of KIPP NorCal’s mission, vision and how it comes to life in our schools; commitment to equity, and organizational goals to develop and oversee impactful communications across internal and external stakeholder groups.
* Ensure the KIPP NorCal brand and visual identity is reflected and reinforced in print and digital collateral, and across schools and the RSO.
* Design touchpoints and build systems to ensure internal and external stakeholders are inspired by our 2030 vision and goals, draw connections to how those translate to annual initiatives and key results, and are informed of progress at a regular cadence.
* Lead coordinated communications and media relations during critical or sensitive moments, strengthening reputation, stakeholder trust, and leadership effectiveness.
* Provide input on KIPP NorCal's public positions, lead media relations, and serve as the organization’s spokesperson as needed.
* Coordinate and collaborate with the KIPP Foundation and other KIPP regions on cross\-regional campaigns, knowledge sharing, and best practices.

 **Executive Affairs and CEO Office Effectiveness**

* Set direction on the RSO's organizational rhythm, including the strategy and design of all\-staff meetings, leadership events, and culture practices, so staff feel connected to the mission and to each other.
* Oversee CEO communications (i.e. speeches, all\-staff emails, talking points, internal and external presentations) to ensure they are high quality and reflect clear messaging, differentiated by stakeholder group.
* Produce and oversee cascading communications for key updates and announcements.
* Oversee strategy to ensure CEO visibility and relationship\-building across internal and external audiences.
* Provide strategic planning support to the CEO as needed, i.e. content creation for leadership team meetings and offsites.

 **Board Governance \& Operations**

* Design and oversee strategy for effective Board governance practices, ensuring that board governance systems are executed with a high degree of quality and fidelity.
* Oversee the process to deliver high\-quality, compliant board packets.
* Ensure quarterly board meetings are engaging, effective, and compliant.
* Serve as staff lead for the Board Governance Council, leading the annual rhythm of governance activities including new board member recruitment.

**Team Leadership and Talent Development**

* Manage a three\-person team, setting clear expectations, monitoring performance, and making timely promotion and exit decisions.
* Build a strong team culture of continuous learning, strengths\-based coaching, and productive collaboration, ensuring each team member has the development support and stretch challenges needed to grow.
* Shape department systems for project management, planning, and progress monitoring so the team operates with clarity and consistency.
* Own and allocate the department’s annual budget, making strategic resource decisions that steward organizational resources wisely.
* Other duties as assigned.

### **KIPP Leadership Competencies**


These competencies describe the core leadership behaviors expected at this level – they guide how we hire, develop, and grow our people at KIPP

new_jobs.description:
*Community Bridges envisions a thriving community where every person has the opportunity to unleash the**ir full potential.* *Our family of ten programs delivers essential services, provides equitable access to resources, and advocates for health and dignity across every stage of life.*

**POSITION DESCRIPTION:**

Under supervision of the CEO and with the support of a cross functional team, the board of directors, and management team, the director of communications \& external affairs (DCEA) implements the organization’s strategic direction for internal and external communications, philanthropy, public affairs, advocacy, government relations, and community engagement. The director implements comprehensive, integrated fundraising and communications strategy consisting of identification, engagement, cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship of current and prospective donors and funders to sustain and expand financial support and connection to the work of Community Bridges (CB). The director manages communications and development staff to execute the coordination and implementation of Community Bridges’ overall fundraising, relationship management, communications, advocacy, and external affairs activities. The (DCEA) performs additional duties as assigned.


**DUTIES \& RESPONSIBILITIES:**

***Agency and Department Leadership:***

* Under the direction of the CEO, develops and oversees the organization's annual communications and philanthropy plans, establishing strategic priorities that support Community Bridges' mission and organizational goals.
* In partnership with the CEO, leads Community Bridges' public affairs, advocacy, government relations, external communications, and philanthropy efforts, developing and implementing strategies that advance the organization's mission, organizational priorities, and public policy objectives.
* Advises the CEO and executive leadership on communications strategies related to organizational reputation, public policy, legislative initiatives, stakeholder engagement, and emerging issues affecting the agency.
* Coordinates agency\-wide messaging to ensure consistency across programs, advocacy efforts, government relations, philanthropy, and public communications.
* Leads and effectively integrates the work of grants, development, and communications staff to achieve communications and fundraising goals.
* Presents monthly progress to the management team, philanthropy committee, and board of directors. Serves on the agency’s management team and when requested provides trainings on development functions such as communications and marketing practices and principles.
* Works collaboratively with the CEO to strengthen relationships with elected officials, government agencies, funding partners, chambers of commerce, nonprofit associations, healthcare organizations, transportation agencies, school districts, and community stakeholders.




***Marketing and Communications Management:***

* Writes, edits, and produces engaging agency communications, including annual reports, fundraising collateral, press releases, newsletters, and brochures; Works in conjunction with management team and coworkers on the production and distribution of all agency collateral and assets.
* In coordination with the CEO leads public relation efforts and daily communications across the agency, including drafting press releases, media strategy, social media management, and management of press affairs.
* Develops and executes strategic public affairs campaigns that educate policymakers, community leaders, funders, and the public regarding Community Bridges' impact, legislative priorities, and public policy initiatives.
* Drives digital engagement including website, email and social media fundraising strategies by overseeing major changes to the website and maintains engaging image driven design.
* In collaboration with the HR department, manages internal communications with staff to increase engagement, collaboration, and employee satisfaction.
* Develops and implements communication strategies that align with key HR initiatives to support recruitment and retention, which includes developing and maintaining employment value proposition and branding strategy, onboarding, employee recognition, and employee wellness while advising on content, tone, and delivery across multiple communication channels.
* Monitors and assesses the effectiveness of internal communications, using staff feedback and engagement metrics to enhance transparency and improve internal communication practices.

***Fund Development \& Leadership:***


* Leads all fundraising campaigns and supervises the gift acknowledgment, tracking and stewardship process.
* Analyzes fundraising performance using data analytics and reporting tools.
* Responsible for the execution of a customized solicitation and stewardship plans.
* Maintains written office procedures manual for development, communications and marketing activities. Develops procedures, methods and systems as directed.
* Supervises the Community Bridges grants program including the grant writer to identify, select, and prepare grant applications.
* Leads the philanthropy committee, including assisting the CEO with identifying, recommending and recruiting philanthropy committee members, volunteers, board members, and consultants for the philanthropy committee.
* Serves as a liaison to the board for fundraising and communication activities.




***Personnel Management:***

* Ensures a positive, culturally sensitive work environment that supports staff and offers opportunities for effective collaboration, professional development, and staff evaluations; maintains a safe work environment that complies with established HR and CB policies and procedures, regulations and union contracts.
* Supervises staff to ensure the achievement of program objectives, including setting goals and expectations, monitoring progress, providing feedback, and implementing accountability measures.

**POSITION SUMMARY**


Oversees the total operation of a YMCA branch, including membership, marketing, programs, facilities, volunteer and staff development, financial development, community relations and collaborations with community agencies.

**ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS**

* Align the work of staff and volunteers to the branch and Association’s strategic plan.
* Establish and implement an annual operating plan: Working with staff, volunteers, and metropolitan management, develop an overall operating plan for the coming year including goals, timelines and expected results.
* Effectively operate the facility and all program activities: Manage the facility on a daily basis, ensuring that regular activities and special programs run smoothly. Establish appropriate management tracking methods to ensure that the branch performs effectively in all key areas.
* Address Specific Problem Areas: Identify and correct areas in which the branch is under\-performing. Organize task forces as necessary to address these issues.
* Represents and promotes the YMCA in the local community and develops positive working relationships with other organizations, businesses, and governmental entities. Develops, maintains, and models collaborative relationships with community agencies in service delivery area.
* Directs the financial development activities of the branch and achieves fund raising goals.
* Develop and support a year\-round fund raising program that includes the annual current support campaign; donor acquisition activity; stewardship of donors; special events, planning giving and capital gifts.
* Support and engage in a structured major donor cultivation strategy that yields annually an agreed upon minimum number of major gifts ($10,000\) to support branch annual operating, capital, and endowment needs.
* Develop and support a long\-range capital development plan including needs and sources of funds that guarantees maintaining all facilities in a like\-new condition.
* Build the branch endowment by increasing the number of members making an irrevocable gift commitment.
* Oversees the hiring, training, supervision and evaluation of staff.
	+ Recruit and hire staff using screening tools and panel interviews to gain the best possible fit with each opening.
	+ See to it that all staff members have development plans and that funding for training is allocated and used appropriately.
	+ Identify career candidates and work an active plan to support their development and advancement

* Develop and control long and short\-term budgets designed to support the achievement of the strategic plan.

* Lead a Board development effort that builds its capacity to help the branch achieve its strategic goals.
* Ensure that each committee has appropriate staff leadership along with benchmarks and charts of work that are aligned with the strategic plan.
* Enable the board to participate in implementation of the strategic plan by providing information that allows it to monitor progress toward stated goals.
* Facilitate the ongoing evaluation of effectiveness and performance of the Board.
* Fosters a climate of innovation to develop member\-focused programs which support the Y mission, goals, and strategies.
* Ensures the safety and maintenance of high quality facilities, grounds, and equipment.
* Directs branch marketing and communication efforts to effectively communicate benefit to the community.
* Serves as a member of Y management and supports the overall objectives of the YMCA.
* **Attend** staff meetings and trainings as required.
* **Uphold** YMCA policies for safety, supervision, mandated reporting and risk management.
* **Demonstrate** the Six Pillars of Character (Trustworthiness, Citizenship, Respect, Responsibility, Caring, and Fairness) as well as the YMCA Core Values of caring, respect, honesty and responsibility in all dealings with members, guests, volunteers and fellow staff.
* **Demonstrate** competencies in and willingness to develop in the Cause Driven Leadership areas of mission advancement, collaboration, operational effectiveness and personal growth.
* All other duties as assigned by your supervisor

new_jobs.description:
**Interim Chief Advancement Officer\-** this is a **temporary** **position for 6 months**, full\-time (40 hours), exempt, located at our Vicente campus in San Francisco.

  


**Mission:** We provide the people, place, and path for exceptional youth mental healthcare.


**Vision:** We aim to redefine what youth mental healthcare can be by delivering transformative support that doesn't just treat symptoms but changes lives.

  


**About Edgewood:**



Each person who works at Edgewood Center for Children and Families shares a passion for making a difference in the lives of the children and families who we serve in the San Francisco Bay Area. Employees find themselves surrounded by a diverse and extraordinary group of dedicated professionals, in welcoming environments characterized by whole\-person care, open communication, creative support and a commitment to growth, healing and improving each day. Edgewood’s successes come from our employees and the positive results they help create for children and families. When you join Edgewood, you join a culture of purpose and belonging, where your growth is a priority, your identity is embraced, and the work you do matters.

  


**Position Summary**



Edgewood seeks a seasoned, entrepreneurial Advancement leader to serve as Interim Chief Advancement Officer (CAO) for a six\-month engagement. This leader will step in with immediate impact, providing senior executive\-level guidance across the full spectrum of Advancement — fundraising, brand development, communications, marketing, strategy, and innovation — during a critical period of organizational momentum. Reporting directly to the CEO and working in close partnership with the Board of Directors, Executive and Senior Leadership Teams, the Temporary Interim CAO will provide continuity and forward momentum across all Advancement activities. As a member of the Executive Team, this leader will contribute to and help implement Edgewood's strategic plans in partnership with the CEO. This leader will guide the internal Advancement team responsible for Edgewood’s annual fund and a major gifts campaign while simultaneously overseeing brand communications, marketing strategy, digital innovation, and community engagement to strengthen Edgewood's visibility, reputation, and long\-term sustainability. The Interim CAO will bring continuity, coherence, urgency, and strategic clarity to all Advancement area operations during this interim period.

  




**Scope of Leadership**



The Interim CAO will serve as Edgewood's senior strategic voice for:

* Annual fund and campaign team guidance and major gifts strategy

  


**Coordinating existing communications and marketing plans including the current workplans for A Place to Begin (APTB)**:


* Brand strategy, communications, and reputation management
* Specific program targeted marketing
* Integrated marketing across digital, social, earned media, and events channels
* Events leadership
* Digital transformation and innovation (including AI tools, technology systems, and HIPAA\-compliant protocols related to leads and marketing)

Leadership \& Team Management (25%)


* Provide senior executive leadership to the CEO, Edgewood Board, and its Leadership, Advancement, Major Gifts Committees across all fundraising and marketing activities.

* Attract, retain, and mentor top talent; support hiring and onboarding as needed throughout the Department.
* Represent Edgewood in the greater Bay Area community, pursuing both Advancement goals and the broader mission of Edgewood in partnership with the CEO.
* Routinely visit Edgewood's locations and attend events to engage with staff and deepen understanding of programs, services, and impact for children and families.
* Actively partner with colleagues at the Executive and Senior Leadership level, pushing for high standards, effective execution, and strong cross\-departmental communication.



Fundraising \& Revenue Development (30%)


* Provide strategic oversight and coaching to the Advancement team as they execute the annual fund campaign — setting goals, messaging, and engagement strategies to maximize donor participation and revenue.
* Lead strategy and work collaboratively with Major Gifts Officer leading efforts on the $10 million major gifts campaign — with $6\.8 million remaining to be raised — including identification, cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship of major donors.
* Oversee all aspects of the Advancement program: individual, corporate, and foundation giving; estate and planned gifts; special events; grants; and ongoing donor stewardship.
* Oversee Edgewood's annual Advancement budget; explore opportunities to grow revenue across all fundraising channels in collaboration with the CEO, Executive Team, and Advancement Committee.
* Ensure a strong working relationship between the Advancement and Finance Departments, with full monthly reconciliation and close management of cash flow.
* Oversee production of an un\-reconciled Gift Detail Report weekly; with reconciliation with Finance occurring monthly.
